diffCoverage.notes: All eight changed source files reviewed across the impacted layers: types (sticker DTO + events/stored carriers), zalo (mapping + facade wrap), store (schema migration + repositories), command (commands + persistence routing), and the lib.rs command registration.

findings.evidence: Forward-only layering (types → config → store → zalo → session → command, ADR-0003) is preserved. zca-rust types stay confined to the zalo layer: sticker_from_content maps the upstream ZcaMessageContent into the pure types::Sticker DTO, and send_sticker/search_stickers/sticker_category wrap the zca-rust facade and return only core DTOs. The types layer (sticker.rs, events.rs, stored.rs) has no zca-rust dependency. No new layer was added.

The schema migration is stepped (v1→v2 sticker columns on messages, v2→v3 recent_stickers table) using user_version, consistent with the existing migrate() pattern. New repositories (save_sticker_message, record_recent_sticker, load_recent_stickers, load_recent_sticker_categories) use parameterized SQL and keep rusqlite confined to the store layer. The command layer calls these typed methods; no SQL leaks upward.

command depends only backward on session (throttled send_sticker) and store (persistence). The new Tauri commands take non-secret args (account/thread ids, ThreadKind, the Sticker display DTO) and are registered in lib.rs. Sticker packs are derived from recent usage because Zalo exposes no owned-packs endpoint — a documented constraint, not a layering break.

summary: Architecture review: the sticker slice keeps the ADR-0003 forward-only layering intact and confines zca-rust to the zalo layer. Types carry pure DTOs, the store migration is stepped and parameterized, and command depends only backward on session + store. No new layer, no provider bypass. Approve.
